cards.back: When a model is too complex and performs well on the training data but poorly on new, unseen data
Technique used to prevent overfitting by adding a penalty term to the loss function
When a model is too simple and fails to capture the underlying patterns in the data
The tradeoff between the model's ability to fit the training data (bias) and its ability to generalize to new data (variance)
Technique used to evaluate the performance of a model by splitting the data into training and testing sets
